source_name: Code4rena Noya Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: PendleConnector incorrectly sends the redeemed PT tokens to the market
summary: Burning a Pendle LP will donate the PT tokens to the market instead of withdrawing them to the PendleConnector which leads to a loss of funds for the Connector.
root_cause: incorrect accounting or validation logic in PendleConnector incorrectly sends the redeemed PT tokens to the market, causing the protocol to compute or enforce the wrong state transition
broken_invariants: the withdrawal flow path must not allow the condition described by "PendleConnector incorrectly sends the redeemed PT tokens to the market" to change user balances, collateralization, or protocol availability unexpectedly
users must retain a reliable exit path unless an explicitly designed and validated pause or restriction is active
state changes must remain internally consistent across all affected accounting buckets and external integrations
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: Burning a Pendle LP will donate the PT tokens to the market instead of withdrawing them to the PendleConnector which leads to a loss of funds for the Connector.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: function burnLP(IPMarket market, uint256 amount) external onlyManager nonReentrant { IERC20(address(market)).safeTransfer(address(market), amount); - market.burn(address(this), address(market), amount); + market.burn(address(this), address(this), amount); market.skim(); emit BurnLP(address(market), amount); }
